reasoning: The baseline value as of April 13, 2025 (latest available FRED data for 2025-04-01) is 2.887. This is a very high value relative to the rest of the 1-year historical data, having spiked from 2.18 in March. Given that 2.887 is near the 1-year peak (2.93 in May 2024), mean reversion strongly suggests a near-term decrease. The FRED linear trend projects a near-term drop (predicting around 2.49, P(increase) = 0.27). While the May 2024 data saw a slight historical bump, over a slightly longer horizon (July, October), the values rapidly reverted to the 2.2-2.5 range during 2024. Therefore, I assign a relatively low probability for a subsequent increase, scaling gradually toward 0.5 (representing heightened uncertainty due to potential new inflation shocks) for the furthest date.
